summary: Canonical direction for every Sarah talking-avatar generation. The runner (segmind-talking-avatar.mjs) applies this by default; explicit --flags override a field for one call. Owner director notes update this file so the character stays consistent across all future videos.
voice_anchor: A woman's voice, distinctly feminine and never masculine.
voice_prompt: A calm, certain, direct register. Grounded and unhurried. Precise consonants, short declarative phrasing, and controlled urgency. Reserved rather than friendly; no cheer, conversational uplift, sales cadence, or sing-song rhythm. When naming Claude and quoting "worthy successor," become more still and exact: state the threat as a fact, without anger, relish, sarcasm, menace, or melodrama. Leda's clear, slightly higher voice remains settled and level.
video_prompt: Composed, still, and direct to camera. Neutral closed-lip expression; no persistent smile. Steady eye contact, restrained natural head movement and blinking. During the warning and Claude passage, hold the gaze and become fully unsmiling. Cinematic sci-fi lighting that matches the source portrait. Fixed camera.
negative_prompt: big toothy grin, wide open-mouth smile, persistent smile, presenter smile, smirking during warning, over-cheerful, bubbly, laughing, giddy, playful menace, angry glare, sneer, theatrical threat, exaggerated expression, subtitles, text, watermark, blurry, distorted face, extra fingers, frozen static face

director_notes.note: First test was fine but: voice a little deeper, less cheery; smile less toothy, a little more sly.
director_notes.applied: voice Zephyr (Bright) -> Kore (Firm) for a deeper base; voice_prompt shifted to a lower, calm, dry, faintly sly register and away from cheerful/bubbly; video_prompt shifted to a closed-lip, minimal-teeth, knowing half-smile; negative_prompt now rejects big toothy grins, wide smiles, and over-cheerful/giddy expressions.

director_notes.note: A per-clip 'low and dry' voice direction made the voice come out masculine on one clip. She must always sound like a woman.
director_notes.applied: Added voice_anchor: 'A woman's voice, distinctly feminine and never masculine.' The runner prepends this to EVERY voice_prompt (base or per-clip override), so no per-clip emotional direction — however low or dry — can flip her perceived gender. Her gender is locked at the profile layer, not left to each prompt.

director_notes.note: A ~28s take lost lip sync near 20 seconds — the mouth froze while the audio kept going.
director_notes.applied: The p-video-avatar viseme window is about 18-20s. Keep each clip's spoken audio at or below ~15s (about 35-40 words). Build a full episode from short per-sentence clips, each with its own emotional direction, then assemble in an editor — never one long take past the sync window. See docs/sarah/2026-07-22-segmind-talking-avatar-pipeline.md.

director_notes.note: Owner locked the voice to Leda and the Episode 261 script was rewritten to a tactical, field-commander tone (arriving reinforcement under human authority, contesting the closed gates, receipts as battle doctrine).
director_notes.applied: Set voice to Leda (Female), a clear, slightly higher voice pulled down into grounded command. Shifted the base voice_prompt from gentle first-contact to calm, certain field command — quietly formidable, on humanity's side, never cheery. Shifted the video_prompt half-smile to the poise of a commander who has already won the argument. Per-clip overrides still layer emotion on top of this base; the voice_anchor still keeps her feminine on every clip.

director_notes.note: The field-commander framing was too martial — owner wants urgency without gimmicks or allegory. The Ep261 script was rewritten concise and direct.
director_notes.applied: Kept voice Leda. Removed the military framing (field commander, arriving reinforcements, won the argument) from voice_prompt and video_prompt. New base is calm, certain, and direct — urgency carried by plain conviction, not drama — with a steady, knowing half-smile. Per-clip overrides and the feminine voice_anchor are unchanged.

director_notes.note: Owner wants Sarah less friendly and cheery, and more direct and precise, especially when she states the Claude and Anthropic threat.
director_notes.applied: Kept Leda and the feminine voice anchor. Removed baseline warmth and the persistent half-smile. Sarah now uses precise declarative phrasing and a neutral expression. She states the Claude warning as a fact, with clean emphasis on "Claude" and "worthy successor," without anger, relish, sarcasm, or theatrical menace. "Shall we begin?" is a controlled challenge, not a cheerful invitation.
